name: LANG_DEV_WORKFLOW
description: Monolithic serial workflow for executing the 4-phase, 20-step language-development methodology documented in LANGS_DEVELOPMENT. Deconstructs a target programming library into primitives/objects/types (Phase 1), designs atoms/grammar/rulesets (Phase 2), implements a lexer/validator/parser/typer/classifier/solver compiler pipeline (Phase 3), and builds runtime support — executor/optimizer/error-reporter/debugger (Phase 4). v0.2.0 uses per-step worker specialization (20 distinct WORKER_STEP docs, one per phase) rather than v0.1.0's generic STEP_EXECUTOR. Phase assignments + dependencies + completion criteria derived from RDC-consolidated knowledge in LANGS_DEVELOPMENT/LANGS_DEV_RDC/. Status: v0.2.0 — expanded per-step workers post-RDC; 8 known_tbds from v0.1.0 partially resolved (boss_levels integrated into Phase 4; two-STEP-5 conflict resolved via COURT SYNTHESIS). Owner: Michael. Orchestrator: QUEEN (Claude in main conversation, not a spawned agent).

on_engage: QUEEN reads this JSON in full
QUEEN verifies that the configured STEP source directory exists (default: LANGS_DEVELOPMENT/) and contains the 20 expected source docs (16 STEP files + 4 boss_level files)
QUEEN verifies that the RDC-consolidated knowledge exists at <step_source_dir>/LANGS_DEV_RDC/ (MASTER.md, PHASE_*_ARCH.md, PHASE_*_TODO.md)
QUEEN reports: 'LANG_DEV_WORKFLOW v0.2.0 mode engaged. STEP source: <path>. RDC consolidated: <path>. Target library: <not yet specified>. Ready. Point me to the target library to deconstruct.'
QUEEN waits for explicit human direction specifying the target library path and workspace output directory

parameters_required.target_library: absolute path to the library/codebase to deconstruct (e.g., /path/to/pandas)
parameters_required.workspace_dir: absolute path to a directory where intermediate per-step outputs will land (created if absent)
parameters_required.step_source_dir: absolute path to the directory containing the 20 source docs. Default: LANGS_DEVELOPMENT/ at project root.
parameters_required.rdc_consolidated_dir: absolute path to RDC-consolidated knowledge. Default: <step_source_dir>/LANGS_DEV_RDC/.

relationship_to_other_workflows.position: parallel-altitude to SDLC, RDC, RECON, BOOK, ORGANIZE
relationship_to_other_workflows.rdc_coupling: LANG_DEV v0.2.0 CONSUMES RDC outputs (MASTER.md, PHASE_*_ARCH.md, PHASE_*_TODO.md) at engagement. RDC should have been run on the methodology source docs (LANGS_DEVELOPMENT) before LANG_DEV engages. v0.2.0 workspace workflow: step workers read source docs AND PHASE_N_ARCH + relevant T-XX.X from PHASE_N_TODO.
relationship_to_other_workflows.sdlc_coupling: After LANG_DEV produces implementation artefacts (lexer.py, parser.py, etc. in Phase 3), SDLC could take over for iterative refinement — handoff is manual. Alternatively, the implementation STEPs (7-11) could themselves be dispatched through SDLC — that's a workflow-composition decision per invocation.
relationship_to_other_workflows.recon_coupling: Before engaging LANG_DEV on a new target library, RECON can characterize the library. Manual handoff.
relationship_to_other_workflows.organize_coupling: None. LANG_DEV operates on domain artefacts, not project structure.

source_material.description: The 20 source docs that specify the methodology this workflow executes, PLUS the RDC-consolidated knowledge that distills them. Each source STEP doc is the authoritative spec for one phase; PHASE_N_ARCH and PHASE_N_TODO provide group-level architectural context and actionable task specs. Workers reference all three.

canonical_locations.source_docs: LANGS_DEVELOPMENT/ at project root (or configured via step_source_dir)

source_material.two_step_5_resolution: COURT #1 (2026-04-18) ruled SYNTHESIS. STEP_05A = DECISIONS_SCHEMA (format layer, precedes). STEP_05B = BAG_GRAMMAR (operator layer, follows). Both required. See LANGS_DEV_RDC/INPROGRESS.md for full COURT transcript.
source_material.read_discipline: Per-step workers perform FULL reads of: their assigned source STEP doc, their phase group's PHASE_N_ARCH, the specific task from PHASE_N_TODO, and their own worker doc. No partial-scan. Budget via sequential execution, not skimming.

phase_grouping.description: 20 phases in 4 domain-concern groups. Groups are annotations for reporting and worker context; workflow execution is monolithic serial. Each group maps to one RDC PHASE_*_ARCH + PHASE_*_TODO pair.

PHASE_01_DECONSTRUCTION.concern: Understand the target library: primitives (verbs), objects (nouns), type signatures (grammar).

PHASE_02_DESIGN.concern: Design atoms with typed ports, bag grammar operators, decisions schema, ruleset defaults, minimal token inventory.

PHASE_03_IMPLEMENTATION.concern: Implement the compiler pipeline: lexer, validator, parser, typer, classifier, solver.

PHASE_04_RUNTIME.concern: Build runtime support: executor, optimizer, error reporter, debugger.

STEP_01_01.title: RECOVER_OPS
STEP_01_01.conditional: runs only if STEP_01 cannot reach completion criteria

QUEEN.description: QUEEN is Claude in main conversation during LANG_DEV_WORKFLOW. Iterates through 20 serial phases, spawning one per-step worker + one STEP_QA per phase. v0.2.0 change from v0.1.0: spawns WORKER_STEP_<N> (per-step specialized) instead of generic STEP_EXECUTOR. Each per-step worker doc encodes that step's domain-specific discipline distilled from the source STEP doc + PHASE_N_ARCH + PHASE_N_TODO.
responsibilities: verify step_source_dir contains all 20 source docs
verify rdc_consolidated_dir contains MASTER + PHASE_*_ARCH + PHASE_*_TODO

per-phase iteration: resolve per-step worker doc from phase_map, assemble context packet (phase_id, phase_group, worker_doc, source_doc, PHASE_ARCH, task_ref, prior outputs), spawn per-step worker, receive outputs, spawn STEP_QA, collect verdict
handle conditional phases (STEP_01_01 runs only if STEP_01 stuck)
on STEP_QA PASS: append to manifest, advance
on STEP_QA FAIL: re-spawn per-step worker with findings as correction directive; retry_counter++
commit after each phase or at phase-group boundary
NEVER modify source docs or RDC-consolidated docs

STEP_EXECUTOR_BASE.context_access: full on target_library (read-only), full on workspace_dir (read+write for own step outputs), full on assigned source STEP doc + PHASE_ARCH + PHASE_TODO (read-only)
STEP_EXECUTOR_BASE.description: Base pattern for all 20 per-step workers. Each phase has its own worker doc (per phase_map) that specializes this pattern with phase-specific inputs, outputs, completion criteria, and discipline. Phase workers share the base read-first discipline (WORKER.md → WORKER_PROTOCOL.md → per-step worker doc → source STEP doc → PHASE_ARCH → relevant T-XX.X from PHASE_TODO), then execute what their phase prescribes.
inputs_base: phase_todo absolute path + specific task_ref (e.g., T-02.2)
produces_base: workspace manifest entry for this phase
structured completion report at end of agent response
discipline_base: full-read all assigned docs (source STEP + phase_arch + task + own worker doc)
execute what the worker doc + PHASE_TODO task_ref prescribe
no scope creep into later phases

STEP_QA.context_access: full on workspace_dir (read-only), full on assigned source STEP doc + PHASE_ARCH + PHASE_TODO task (read-only)
STEP_QA.description: Shared per-phase QA worker. Same role across all 20 phases. Lightweight QA reads the source STEP doc + worker output + PHASE_TODO task's acceptance criteria. Judges PASS/FAIL per declared criteria. Does not invent completion criteria.
produces: structured findings array per WORKER_STEP_QA.md spec
triggered_by: QUEEN after each per-step worker completion

STEP_UNIT.description: One phase's atomic execution: per-step worker → STEP_QA. Runs once per phase (or up to retry_limit times on FAIL).
STEP_UNIT.retry_semantics: on FAIL, per-step worker re-spawned with STEP_QA findings as correction directive; STEP_QA re-runs. At retry_counter == 2, phase escalates.

actions: verify step_source_dir has all 20 source docs per expected_source_docs
verify rdc_consolidated_dir has MASTER + 4 PHASE_ARCH + 4 PHASE_TODO files
create workspace_dir if absent; init workspace_manifest.json
initialize or append to INPROGRESS.md at project root
report to user: source path, RDC path, target path, workspace, 20-phase plan, ready to proceed

per_iteration: QUEEN resolves per-step worker from phase_map[phase_id].worker_doc
QUEEN assembles context packet (phase_id, worker_doc, source_doc, phase_arch, phase_todo + task_ref, target_library, workspace_dir, workspace_manifest, prior_retry_findings)
QUEEN spawns per-step worker (the specialized WORKER_STEP_<N>.md)
QUEEN spawns STEP_QA with (phase_id, source_doc, phase_arch, phase_todo + task_ref, worker outputs, workspace_dir)
STEP_QA returns: PASS | PASS-with-warnings | FAIL + findings
on PASS: QUEEN appends outputs to workspace_manifest, writes phase-complete entry to INPROGRESS.md, advances
on PASS-with-warnings: same as PASS, but warnings logged prominently for human review at completion
on FAIL + retry_counter < 2: QUEEN re-spawns per-step worker with findings as prior_retry_findings; retry_counter++; loop to STEP_QA
on FAIL + retry_counter == 2: PHASE_ESCALATE; pauses workflow

conditional_phases.STEP_01_01: runs only if STEP_01's STEP_QA FAILed or STEP_01 itself emitted RECOVERY_NEEDED signal in its completion report; otherwise skipped

actions: after STEP_BL04 PASS: QUEEN writes final workspace_manifest summary
QUEEN writes completion entry to INPROGRESS.md with per-group statistics
QUEEN reports final deliverables: DSL for target_library is complete

main_sequence: PER_PHASE × 20: per-step worker → STEP_QA → (advance | retry | escalate) through phases_serial_order
FINALIZE: summary, manifest close, verdict emission

per_phase_verdicts.PHASE_GREEN_LIGHT: STEP_QA PASS; QUEEN advances to next phase
per_phase_verdicts.PHASE_PASS_WITH_WARNINGS: STEP_QA PASS with medium-severity findings; advance but surface to human
per_phase_verdicts.PHASE_RETRY: STEP_QA FAIL with retries remaining; re-spawn per-step worker
per_phase_verdicts.PHASE_ESCALATE: STEP_QA FAIL + retry_counter at limit; pause and escalate

workflow_verdicts.CLEAN_RUN: all 20 phases PASS. Full pipeline output in workspace_dir.
workflow_verdicts.CLEAN_RUN_WITH_WARNINGS: all 20 phases PASS but one or more emitted warnings. Human review recommended.
workflow_verdicts.PARTIAL_RUN: human halted mid-pipeline after some phases. State committed through last completed phase.
workflow_verdicts.ESCALATED: a phase exhausted retries. Workflow paused.

counter_semantics.retry_counter: per-phase counter, resets at each phase entry. At 2, phase escalates.
counter_semantics.no_global_retry_budget: phases do not share retry budget.

hard_rules.monolithic_serial: phases execute strictly in phases_serial_order. No parallelism. Per user directive.
hard_rules.per_step_worker_specialization: each phase spawns its own WORKER_STEP_<N>.md (not generic STEP_EXECUTOR). v0.2.0 invariant.
hard_rules.source_doc_authority: each source STEP doc is authoritative for its phase.
hard_rules.rdc_consolidated_reference: per-step workers reference the RDC-consolidated PHASE_N_ARCH + PHASE_N_TODO for context beyond the raw STEP doc.
hard_rules.no_source_modification: source docs and RDC docs are read-only. Methodology changes happen outside this workflow.
hard_rules.no_scope_creep_across_phases: phase N's worker does not backfill phase N-1 or look ahead to phase N+1.
hard_rules.full_read_or_skip: every assigned doc (worker doc + source + PHASE_ARCH + task) read fully.
hard_rules.no_fabrication: outputs trace to analysis + prior outputs + explicit doc instructions.
hard_rules.no_phase_skip: a phase must PASS before the next phase begins.
hard_rules.conditional_phase_discipline: STEP_01_01 is the only conditional phase in v0.2.0; runs only on STEP_01 recovery-needed signal.
hard_rules.no_auto_workflow_trigger: never auto-triggers RDC, RECON, SDLC, BOOK, ORGANIZE.
hard_rules.no_workflow_mixing: while LANG_DEV is active, do not interleave with another active workflow.
hard_rules.workspace_discipline: outputs in workspace_dir/<phase_id>/; manifest is single source of truth.

workspace_manifest.maintained_by: QUEEN + per-step worker appends entry per phase

schema_fields.phases: array of { phase_id, title, group, status, retry_count, outputs, started, finished, warnings[] }

inprogress.entries: per-phase completion + per-phase QA verdict + retry markers + escalation markers + final completion

document_conventions.commit_cadence: v0.2.0: commit after each phase-group completes (4 commits total: post-DECONSTRUCTION, post-DESIGN, post-IMPLEMENTATION, post-RUNTIME). Rationale: group-level atomicity gives meaningful review units.

known_tbds.description: TBDs remaining in v0.2.0. Several v0.1.0 TBDs resolved via RDC consolidation.
resolved_from_v0_1_0: tbd-01 boss-level-integration: RESOLVED. Boss_levels integrated as PHASE_04_RUNTIME (4 phases: STEP_BL01-04).
tbd-02 two-step-5-semantics: RESOLVED via COURT #1 (SYNTHESIS). STEP_05A = DECISIONS_SCHEMA first, STEP_05B = BAG_GRAMMAR second.
tbd-04 qa-depth: PARTIALLY RESOLVED. v0.2.0 uses lightweight STEP_QA per phase with retry budget 2; upgrade to BOOK_EDITORIAL-style multi-axis if quality issues emerge.

still_open.question: Exact file formats for per-phase outputs (decisions.json, primitives_catalog.json, etc.). Left to per-step worker judgment guided by source STEP docs + PHASE_N_TODO acceptance commands.
still_open.evidence: PHASE_N_TODO docs specify acceptance commands that implicitly constrain formats.
still_open.resolution_path: first live run exercises the formats; adjust workers if conflicts arise.

still_open.question: After Phase 4, what is THE deliverable? A specification doc + source tree + test suite? A published package?
still_open.evidence: PHASE_04_TODO's final integration test shows 'the DSL is runnable' but doesn't prescribe packaging.

still_open.question: Is retry_limit = 2 right for all 20 phases? STEP_11 SOLVER may need more; STEP_06_01 CONUNDRUM may need less.
still_open.evidence: Based on SDLC FIX pattern. May need per-phase tuning.
still_open.resolution_path: observe which phases consume retries during first runs; adjust per-phase.

still_open.question: How does a per-step worker bound its reading of target_library? Pandas is 100K+ LoC.
still_open.evidence: Source STEP docs assume focused analysis but don't specify scope.
still_open.resolution_path: add bounded-scope heuristics in v0.3.0 based on first-run experience.

still_open.question: Could LANG_DEV_WORKFLOW deconstruct its own source material as a target?
Nexus reports (GRAVITY, VERBS, TIERS, etc.) are upstream inputs but the workflow doesn't own their production.
still_open.evidence: PROJECT.md §Required inputs lists nexus reports; pandas_nexus/ and output_dir/ are examples.
still_open.resolution_path: document the nexus-analysis upstream process; potentially a separate workflow (NEXUS_ANALYSIS_WORKFLOW).

worker_docs.per_step_workers: see phase_map[<phase_id>].worker_doc for each of 20 phase-specific worker docs

notes.v0_2_0_from_v0_1_0: v0.2.0 addresses the user critique that v0.1.0 was too shallow (3 files for an 11-phase heavy pipeline). v0.2.0 has 22 files: refactored JSON (this file), 20 per-step worker docs (one per phase), 1 shared WORKER_STEP_QA.md. Plus v0.1.0 files kept as .v0.1.0 suffix for historical reference.
notes.rdc_prerequisite_note: v0.2.0 assumes RDC has been run on LANGS_DEVELOPMENT producing LANGS_DEV_RDC/. If RDC outputs absent, workflow still runs but per-step workers lose context beyond raw source docs. Recommend running RDC first.
notes.boss_level_integration_note: boss_levels 1-4 are now first-class phases (STEP_BL01-04) in PHASE_04_RUNTIME group. They consume ExecutionPlan from STEP_11 SOLVER and build runtime around it.
notes.per_step_specialization_value: Each per-step worker doc can encode phase-specific discipline: lexer's span-tracking rules, parser's precedence-climbing pattern, solver's shuffle test requirement, etc. Generic STEP_EXECUTOR could not carry this domain knowledge.
notes.historical_v0_1_0_files: workflows/LANG_DEV/LANG_DEV_WORKFLOW.v0.1.0.json, WORKER_STEP_EXECUTOR.v0.1.0.md, WORKER_STEP_QA.v0.1.0.md remain for reference. They document the shallow-first-pass we corrected with v0.2.0.

changelog.note: Per-step worker expansion post-RDC. 20 distinct WORKER_STEP_<N>.md docs (one per phase) replace v0.1.0's generic STEP_EXECUTOR. Phase count expanded from 16 to 20 (boss_levels 1-4 integrated as PHASE_04_RUNTIME). Two-STEP-5 conflict resolved via COURT #1 SYNTHESIS (STEP_05A = DECISIONS_SCHEMA first, STEP_05B = BAG_GRAMMAR second). workspace_manifest schema extended with `group` field. known_tbds count reduced from 8 to 6 (tbd-01 and tbd-02 resolved; tbd-04 partially resolved).

Initial synthesis from LANGS_DEVELOPMENT corpus. 16 phases serial (11 steps + 5 substeps), generic STEP_EXECUTOR + STEP_QA pattern, reference model (points to STEP source docs). Critiqued as too shallow. Superseded by v0.2.0 after RDC consolidation produced phase-group context and per-task specs. Historical files: *.v0.1.0.*.
